explanation: This command simulates a suspicious shim database installation using sdbinst.exe with an uncommon extension (e.g., .sdb). It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.

This command adds a common flag (-f) to simulate a legitimate-looking command line, but still includes the suspicious .sdb extension. It may bypass detection if the rule does not account for additional flags.
This command includes a common flag (-c) to blend in with legitimate usage patterns. It may bypass detection if the rule does not account for reordering or additional flags.
This command adds a common flag (-t) to simulate a legitimate command line. It may bypass detection if the rule does not account for additional flags.
This command includes a combination of flags (-m -bg) that are considered legitimate by the Sigma rule's filter. It may bypass detection if the rule does not account for these combinations.
This command includes multiple -i flags to simulate a more complex command line. It may bypass detection if the rule does not account for multiple instances of the same flag.
